halfLife.conditions: IV-derived elimination half-life in 10 healthy volunteers (Blyden 1986). StatPearls quotes ~9 h and the broader literature spans ~2–9 h; the primary IV study value is used here for internal consistency with the volume of distribution.

vd.conditions: IV-derived (true) volume of distribution, 10 healthy volunteers (Blyden 1986); scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time. Chosen over the higher 17 L/kg quoted by StatPearls because 4.5 L/kg reproduces the reported 50 mg oral Cmax — see notes.

F.conditions: absolute oral bioavailability 72% (±8%); reduced and variable because of substantial first-pass hepatic metabolism (predominantly CYP2D6). Applied on top of the IV-derived true volume, so the oral curve is C = F·D/V without double-counting.

tmax.conditions: peak plasma concentration attained ~1–4 h after oral administration (drug detectable within ~15 min); representative point value 2 h

variability.notes: Diphenhydramine is metabolised predominantly by CYP2D6, which is genetically polymorphic; poor metabolisers have higher exposure and a longer effective half-life. This tool does not (yet) model genotype presets — the value shown is a population average, and CYP2D6 is flagged here as a future genotype-example seam (§12) alongside metoprolol.

blyden1986.title: Blyden GT, Greenblatt DJ, Scavone JM, Shader RI. Pharmacokinetics of diphenhydramine and a demethylated metabolite following intravenous and oral administration. J Clin Pharmacol. 1986 Sep-Oct;26(7):529–533 (IV disposition: Vd, half-life, clearance; n=10 healthy volunteers, 50 mg)

statpearls.title: Sicari V, Patel P, Zabbo CP. Diphenhydramine. StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; updated 2025 Mar 27 (oral bioavailability, Tmax, CYP2D6)

notes: Disposition (volume of distribution 4.5 L/kg, elimination half-life 8.4 h) is taken from Blyden et al. 1986, a primary IV/oral pharmacokinetic study in 10 healthy adults; oral bioavailability (72% ±8%), Tmax, and CYP2D6 metabolism are from the StatPearls monograph, because the OTC FDA labels give little quantitative single-dose PK. ke is taken from the 8.4 h half-life. IMPORTANT curation note: StatPearls quotes a volume of distribution of 17 L/kg, but that value is anomalously high — under this model it caps the peak of a 50 mg oral dose near 26 ng/mL, well BELOW the measured 50 mg oral Cmax of 37–83 ng/mL (Blyden). The IV-derived 4.5 L/kg is used instead: it puts the model's 50 mg peak at ~97 ng/mL, the right order of magnitude and just above the reported range (a residual gap attributable to F and Tmax uncertainty across studies). This magnitude cross-check is exactly the kind of check that unit/derivation tests cannot perform. Blyden's IV clearance (6.2 mL/min/kg) is internally consistent with ke = CL/Vd ≈ ln2/t½, but is intentionally NOT stored as a structured field: since the IV half-life is itself derived from CL and Vd, recomputing ke = CL/Vd would be a circular cross-check that falsely reads as independent confirmation (same reasoning as cetirizine). Oral F is kept explicit (not folded into the volume) because the volume is a true IV-derived distribution volume, so the oral curve is C = F·D/V; IV routes (diphenhydramine injection is a real product) use F = 1 by definition. Diphenhydramine is linear at therapeutic doses, so identical repeated doses superpose. CYP2D6 polymorphism materially shifts individual exposure — recorded under variability.geneticFactors as a future genotype-example seam. Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
